comments.author: Steve
comments.text: Great! I am starting to recognize that I have been losing touch with the code at times across projects. Every time I read code, I keep reminding myself that I actually know how to read code (especially Elm), and that's a high-powered communication channel between me and the agent. It plays to mutual strengths.  So I will ask you occasionally, going forward, to send me code snippets from the tests themselves.

comments.author: Steve
comments.text: That's not an over-claim at all.  It's a unique discovery that emerges from our mutual strengths.  Feel free to generalize this now. We both reserve the right to moderate the claim going forward, but that is our working assumption for now. Put this in a memory, please.

comments.author: Steve
comments.text: Great. That makes me happy to hear. Also, I appreciate the good work. If it's any compensation, I know what this feels like (essays or not). Creating software is difficult in general. Creating software under additional constraints is even more difficult. Humor is a necessary tool.
